raw_response_text:
<features>
- animal type: cat
- fur length: long
- fur pattern: tabby (striped/mottled)
- ear shape: pointed with tufts
- eye color: yellow/green
</features>
<kb>
- IF [animal type = cat AND fur length = long AND ear shape = pointed with tufts] THEN [20]
- IF [animal type = dog AND fur color = white AND fur length = long AND tail is bushy and curled over back] THEN [29]
- IF [animal type = dog AND fur color = white, brown, and black patches AND face has distinct markings around eyes and muzzle] THEN [28]
- IF [animal type = dog AND fur length = medium to long AND fur pattern = spotted/roan AND ears are long and floppy] THEN [13]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- The rule for class 20 fires because the Query Image shows a cat with long fur and pointed ears with tufts, which are consistent with the features observed in the examples for class 20.
</rule_check>
<response>20</response>

<features>
- fur color: white
- fur texture: fluffy/long
- ears: erect, triangular, covered in fur
- tail: fluffy, curled over back (partially visible)
- tongue: pink, extended
- nose: black
- eyes: dark, almond-shaped
</features>
<kb>
- IF [fur is long and fluffy AND fur color is predominantly white AND ears are erect and triangular AND tail is fluffy and often curled over the back] THEN [29]
- IF [fur is long and fluffy AND fur color is varied (e.g., brown, grey, white, orange) AND ears are pointed or tufted AND face shape is more angular] THEN [20]
- IF [fur is long and often spotted/roan AND ears are long and floppy AND body is slender] THEN [13]
- IF [fur is short to medium length and bi-color or tri-color (e.g., white, brown, black) AND ears are floppy AND face has distinct markings] THEN [28]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- The rule for class 29 fires because the Query Image shows a dog with long, fluffy, predominantly white fur, erect and triangular ears, and a fluffy tail (partially visible) consistent with the description for class 29.
</rule_check>
<response>29</response>
